text.title: Rust Ownership Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns: cloning to silence the checker, RefCell-as-default, str/String confusion, missing lifetimes on trait returns.

antipattern.symptom: `.clone()` appears on every line of a function. Profiler shows the function spends 40% of CPU in `String::clone`.

root-cause.source: Internal — observed in Rust-newcomer code Q4 2025

antipattern.root-cause: Author writes the body first, hits the borrow checker, reaches for `.clone()` as the universal "fix". Each clone hides a signature problem (should have been `&str` not `String`).
antipattern.fix: Re-audit signatures using the 3-question rule. Convert `String` → `&str`, `Vec` → `&[T]`. Run `cargo clippy -- -W clippy::redundant_clone` to surface cheap removable clones.

antipattern.symptom: `Rc<RefCell<T>>` wraps half the structs in the crate. Runtime panics: "already borrowed: BorrowMutError".
antipattern.root-cause: RefCell defers borrow checks to runtime. When the data shape is actually tree-like (not graph), RefCell adds runtime cost + bug surface without solving the actual aliasing problem.
antipattern.fix: Restructure to ownership tree: parent owns children; children reference parent by id, not by `Weak`. State machines: enum + ownership move. Use Rc/RefCell only for genuine graphs with cycles.

antipattern.symptom: Function takes `String` parameter; every caller does `s.to_string()` even though they have `&str`. Caller benchmark shows 30% of time in string allocation.
antipattern.root-cause: Author defaulted to owned types thinking that was "safer". Owned types force allocation at every call site.
antipattern.fix: Change parameter to `&str` if function only reads. If it stores, take `impl Into<String>` to let caller pass owned or borrowed.

antipattern.symptom: `fn iter() -> impl Iterator<Item = &Foo>` rejected with `missing lifetime specifier`. Author adds `'static` to silence.

root-cause.source: Rust Reference ch.10 — Lifetime Elision Rules

antipattern.root-cause: Trait return position is not covered by elision rules. `'static` works but forces callers to also pin `'static`, propagating the constraint.
antipattern.fix: Write explicit lifetime: `fn iter<'a>(&'a self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &'a Foo> + 'a`. Comment what outlives what.
